seoTitle: Change QuickBooks' Default Bank Account Selection
metaDescription: Stop QuickBooks Online from pulling funds from the wrong account by setting default banking preferences and automation rules.
intro: Accidentally drawing funds from the wrong account on every entry is a preventable annoyance. This guide shows how to configure default bank account behavior in QuickBooks Online so forms consistently pull from the right place.

steps.heading: Open global expense settings
steps.body: Click the gear icon, select Account and Settings, then go to the Advanced tab and find the Automation section.

steps.heading: Enable pre-fill preferences
steps.body: Click the edit pencil icon and turn on pre-fill options so the system auto-selects your last used bank account on forms.

steps.heading: Save the global setting
steps.body: Click Save, then Done, to lock in this behavior for expense forms.

steps.heading: Set sales deposit defaults
steps.body: Open a Sales Receipt or Receive Payment form and change the Deposit To dropdown from Undeposited Funds to your core checking account.

steps.heading: Automate specific vendors with rules
steps.body: Go to Transactions, then Rules, and create a rule to route specific supplier charges automatically to a target account.

faq.question: Can QuickBooks remember my last used bank account automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, enabling pre-fill preferences in Advanced settings lets QuickBooks auto-select your last used account on new forms.

faq.question: How do I stop sales deposits from going to Undeposited Funds?
faq.answer: Change the Deposit To dropdown on your Sales Receipt or Receive Payment form to your checking account instead.

faq.question: Can I set different default accounts for different vendors?
faq.answer: Yes, use Bank Feed Rules to automatically route specific supplier charges to a designated account.

faq.question: Will these settings apply to all users on my account?
faq.answer: Some settings are account-wide while others are user-specific, so review your team's needs when adjusting multi-user preferences.
